page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 physical cores on an ARM64 architecture utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system provides 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25%, whereas single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, fall into the weak and average categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average tier, with read-only large block bandwidth at 163.72 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 78.22 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression recovers to an average tier.

The combination of a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and a large local NVMe SSD makes this instance profile suitable for memory-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space, such as database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the average tier. However, the instance exhibits poor performance in large language model text generation, particularly with 135M and 70B models, indicating it is not optimal for heavy LLM inference tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture offers a high density of dedicated physical cores and memory without hyperthreading, presenting a balanced resource profile for multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng tests, though single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks remain in the average to weak categories. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform in the average tier. This hardware configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded processing workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage, while showing limitations in single-threaded execution and large language model text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, substantial RAM, and high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Provides 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing
